Program news
Learn what's new with WIC.
Use your fruits and vegetables benefits at farmers' markets with the new WIC card
A new California WIC card with a QR code allows families to shop for fresh produce at the farmers’ markets year-round using their fruits and vegetables WIC benefits.
Here is how it works:
Shop at a WIC authorized farmers’ market. Find one on the
Farmers Market Locations page of the California WIC website
.
Show your WIC card, and the farmer will scan the QR code.
Confirm your purchase by entering your PIN and enjoy fresh local produce.
You can pick up your new card at these WIC offices
Anderson
2889 East Center Street, Suite 102
Anderson, CA 96007
Enterprise
2757 Churn Creek Road, Suite C
Redding, CA 96002
About the Women, Infants and Children Program (WIC)
The Shasta County WIC program helps families eat well, stay healthy, and be active. If you are pregnant or have a child under 5, you may be eligible to receive:
Healthy, no cost foods
Nutrition education for you and your family
Referrals to community services
Breastfeeding information and support
Who is eligible?
WIC serves families with low to medium incomes, including many working families.
If you receive Medi-Cal, CalWORKs or CalFresh (Food Stamps), you could meet the income eligibility for WIC.
U.S. citizenship is not a requirement for receiving WIC. The non-cash benefits provided by WIC do not affect immigration status. WIC is an equal opportunity provider.
How do I find out if I’m eligible?
Test out your eligibility online. Use the
California Department of Public Health’s “Am I Eligible?” tool
, or contact Shasta County WIC.
Call
(530) 225-5168 or (530) 224-4200
Text
Shasta County WIC at (888) 424-6499
Email
ShastaWIC@shastacounty.gov
WIC card and app
Use the WIC Card and WIC App together to make shopping easier.
Learn more about the WIC card on the California WIC website.
WIC shopping guide
The WIC Authorized Food List Shopping Guide is an educational tool that provides information about the foods authorized by WIC.
View the
Authorized Food List Shopping Guide on the California WIC My Family website.
Welcome to WIC video
New to WIC?
Watch the Welcome to WIC video on the My Family WIC website
.
Additional resources for WIC families
Peer Counselors
WIC Breastfeeding Peer Counselors are paraprofessionals who have breastfed their babies and are enthusiastic about helping others be successful with breastfeeding.
To be eligible to have a Breastfeeding Peer Counselor you must be:
Receiving WIC benefits.
Pregnant or have an infant 30 days old or younger
A first-time mom or a mother who had challenges breastfeeding their previous babies
WIC Breastfeeding Peer Counselors connect with each mom on a monthly basis during her pregnancy to answer breastfeeding and pregnancy-related questions, assist with referrals and dispel any breastfeeding myths or fears.
Shasta Mom’s Circle
WIC Breastfeeding Peer Counselors also facilitate small groups called Shasta Moms' Circle. Every month moms and their counselor get together to discuss issues about breastfeeding, parenting and more.
To be eligible to participate in Shasta Moms' Circle you must be pregnant or breastfeeding.
"Circle" groups have become increasingly popular and may have a waiting list. If you are interested, please let your Peer Counselor know that you'd like to join. Any mom can find us on Facebook to ask questions and "chat" with other moms.
View the
Shasta Mom's Circle Facebook page.
Assistance with breast pumps
WIC can provide guidance on obtaining a breast pump and help you learn how to:
Use the pump
Store breast milk
Schedule feedings
Other helpful tips
Assistance is FREE. Call to make an appointment or text (888) 424-6499.
Breastfeeding Warmline:
(530) 245-6466
See Supporting Documents for instructions on how to keep your breast pump kit clean.
Online classes
Complete nutrition education online
.
Find nutrition classes on the WICHealth websit
e.
